articles.title: Amazon has cut 57,000 jobs, and the people who kept theirs are not fine either
articles.summary: Amazon’s cuts have reached a scale that is hard to absorb. The company has laid off more than 57,000 corporate staff since 2022, around 16% of that workforce, CNBC reports. The pace has accelerated sharply. Roughly 16,000 people went in late January, three months after more than 14,000 others, the steepest cuts in the company’s history.

articles.title: The AI race has quietly stopped being about who has the biggest model
